info.title: SaaS Platform API
info.description:
Multi-tenant SaaS platform API.

**Tenancy.** Each organization owns a dedicated database. Endpoints under
"Tenant-scoped" require an `X-Organization` header carrying the organization's
slug or UUID; the caller must be a member of that organization, or the request
is rejected with 403.

**Auth.** Obtain a token from `POST /api/v1/auth/login` and send it as
`Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

**Permissions.** Roles are organization-scoped, so the same user may hold
different permissions in different organizations.

get.description: Every suspend, edit, delete, restore, and purge, newest first. Central and cross-org — distinct from the per-organization activity log that lives inside each tenant database. Filterable by action and by target organization.

404.description: Not a super admin

post.summary: Impersonate a member of an organization
post.description: Returns a short-lived token that acts as the target user (an owner by default, or the named member) inside THIS organization only. The token cannot reach the admin API and cannot enter the target user's other organizations. A super admin can never be impersonated. Both start and stop are written to the admin audit trail.

user_id.description: Member to impersonate; defaults to an owner

token.description: Use as the bearer token to act as the user

404.description: Not a super admin, or unknown organization

422.description: Target is a super admin, not a member, or is yourself

get.description: Super-admin only. Central-context: reads across all organizations without entering any tenant database. Searchable by organization name, phone, or owner name/email; filterable by status, plan, and registration date; sortable on an allowlist. Cross-tenant metrics (projects, storage) are null until the Phase 2 stats rollup lands.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Organization name, phone, or owner name/email

404.description: Not a super admin (the admin surface is not advertised)

get.description: Super-admin only. Organization counts by lifecycle state plus distinct users across the platform. Cross-tenant totals (projects, storage) are null pending the Phase 2 rollup — null means not-yet-measured, distinct from a real zero.

404.description: Not a super admin
Not a super admin, or unknown organization

put.description: The slug is intentionally not editable — it is the identifier clients send in X-Organization, and changing it would break every integration. Status changes go through the suspend/activate endpoints, not this one.

404.description: Not a super admin, or unknown organization

delete.description: Reversible. The central row is trashed — which cuts member access immediately — but the physical tenant database is KEPT, so the org can be restored. The database is only ever dropped later, by the tenants:purge command, after a retention window. A "soft delete" here never destroys data.

404.description: Not a super admin, or unknown organization

put.description: Give one organization different ceilings than its plan without inventing a bespoke plan. The body replaces the whole override map: a key present overrides the plan (an integer, or null for unlimited); a key absent falls back to the plan. Send an empty object to clear all overrides. All quota enforcement — uploads, record creation — honours these immediately.

404.description: Not a super admin, or unknown organization

post.description: Cuts access immediately: the tenant middleware refuses a suspended organization on its next request, so no token needs to expire. Reversible via activate.

404.description: Not a super admin, or unknown organization

post.description: Restores to active, never to trial — reviving a spent trial would grant a second free run.

404.description: Not a super admin, or unknown organization

post.description: Brings a trashed organization and its members back online. Because the database was never dropped, nothing is re-provisioned — the data is exactly as it was left.

404.description: Not a super admin, or no such trashed organization

get.description: Super-admin only. Unlike GET /billing/plans, this includes inactive plans and exposes the Stripe price ids, because an administrator maintains the catalogue rather than shopping from it. Each plan carries organizations_count so the UI can tell which plans are in use — a plan in use cannot be deleted.

404.description: Not a super admin (the admin surface is not advertised)

post.summary: Add a plan to the catalogue
post.description: The slug is derived from the name when omitted, and is the handle billing/subscribe takes. Stripe price ids are optional so a plan can be drafted before its Stripe prices exist — but an interval with no price id cannot be subscribed to, which the response reports as stripe.monthly_ready / stripe.annual_ready. A price id that IS supplied is verified against Stripe (exists, active, recurring, right interval, right currency) and the display amount is then taken from Stripe rather than the submitted value, because Stripe is the source of truth for money. Verification is skipped when no Stripe secret is configured.

404.description: Not a super admin
Unknown plan, or not a super admin

put.description: A partial edit: an absent key is left untouched, while a key sent as null clears it. That distinction matters for the limits, where null means unlimited and 0 means none allowed. Limits take effect on the next request — UsageService reads them per request rather than caching them onto the organization.

404.description: Unknown plan, or not a super admin

delete.summary: Remove a plan from the catalogue
delete.description: Refused with 422 while any organization is on the plan. tenants.plan_id carries no foreign key, so deleting would leave those rows pointing at a plan that no longer exists, and UsageService would silently fall back to the cheapest active plan — re-quotaing them without telling anyone. Deactivate instead: the plan stops being offered while current subscribers keep their limits.

404.description: Unknown plan, or not a super admin

422.description: The plan is still in use

post.summary: Attach a file to a customer
post.description: Executable and script types are refused regardless of the MIME type claimed, because the browser-reported type is attacker-controlled and an .html or .svg served from our origin is stored XSS. The stored path is generated; the original filename is kept only as a label.

422.description: Blocked file type or too large

delete.description: Scoped to the customer, so an id from another record cannot be deleted. Removes the stored bytes as well as the row.

404.description: Not an attachment of this customer

get.description: Read-only by design: an audit log you can edit is not an audit log. Entries live in the tenant database, so the trail is naturally scoped to one organization. Updates record only the fields that actually changed.

get.summary: Verify an email address from a signed link
get.description: Signed rather than authenticated: the link is clicked from a mail client, where no bearer token exists. The signature plus a hash of the current address proves ownership.

post.description: Re-send the verification email to the signed-in user.
Always returns the same message whether or not the address is registered, so this cannot be used to enumerate users. Rate limited to 6/minute.

200.description: Link sent if the address exists

post.description: Returns a token, or 202 with a challenge when the account has two-factor enabled. Five failed attempts per email+IP trigger a 15-minute lockout (429).

device_name.description: Labels the session so it can be reviewed and revoked.

post.description: Revokes only the token that made this request, so other devices stay signed in. Use the sessions endpoints to revoke elsewhere.

get.summary: The authenticated user and the organizations they can switch between
get.description: The authenticated user plus the organizations they can switch between.

put.description: Requires the current password. Every other session is revoked on success, so a password change actually evicts an attacker who already holds a token; the calling device stays signed in.

200.description: Password updated; other devices signed out

post.summary: Register a user and create their first organization
post.description: Creates the user, provisions a dedicated database for the organization, seeds its roles, makes the caller its owner, and returns an API token. Rate limited to 6/minute.

password.description: Min 12 chars with mixed case, a number, and a symbol.

post.summary: Reset a password using an emailed token
post.description: All existing API tokens are revoked: a reset implies the account may have been compromised.

get.description: Lets a user spot unfamiliar devices and revoke them. The current session is flagged.

delete.description: Scoped to your own tokens, so one user cannot sign another out.

404.description: Not one of your sessions

delete.summary: Revoke every session except the current one
delete.description: Revoke every session except the current one.

get.description: Successes and failures for the signed-in user. Paginated.

post.description: Exchanges the challenge_token from a 202 login, plus either a TOTP code or a recovery code, for an API token. Send one of code or recovery_code. A wrong code does not burn the challenge, but five wrong codes destroy it and sign-in restarts from the password. A TOTP code works only once: its time step is recorded and re-presenting it is refused, so observing a code is not enough to reuse it inside its 30-second window.

recovery_code.description: A single-use recovery code; destroyed once spent

recovery_codes_remaining.description: Present when a recovery code was used, to prompt regeneration

422.description: Bad code, or an expired or already-spent challenge

429.description: Too many attempts from this address

get.description: Reports whether enrolment is unstarted, pending confirmation, or active, and how many recovery codes remain. The secret itself is never returned here — only the enable step ever discloses it.

delete.description: Requires the account password: a stolen bearer token must not be enough to remove the factor that exists to survive a stolen password. Clears the secret and every recovery code.

post.description: Returns a secret and an otpauth:// URI to render as a QR code. This does NOT protect the account yet — call confirm with a code from the authenticator to finish. Calling this again discards any half-finished enrolment and issues a fresh secret, so an abandoned QR code cannot be confirmed later.

post.summary: Finish enrolment and get recovery codes
post.description: Proves the authenticator works, and only then does 2FA start guarding sign-in. Recovery codes are returned here and are shown in full exactly once at this point; they are the way back in when the device is lost.

422.description: Wrong code, or enrolment was never started

get.description: Codes are stored encrypted rather than hashed precisely so they can be shown again here — a user who lost the list but still has their authenticator would otherwise have no way to recover one.

post.description: Issues a fresh set and invalidates every previous code — the correct move once a list has been printed, shared, or partially spent. Requires the password.

200.description: New codes issued; old ones are dead

get.description: Amounts are for display only; Stripe is the source of truth for what is actually charged. Stripe price ids are never exposed. A null limit means unlimited, which is different from 0.

200.description: Active plans with limits and features

get.description: Renders entirely from local state: an organization that has never paid has no Stripe customer, and the billing page must not depend on Stripe uptime.

200.description: Subscription state, usage against limits, and the card on file

get.description: Stripe.js uses this to collect card details in the browser. Raw card data never touches our servers, which is what keeps this application out of PCI scope.

post.description: payment_method is a Stripe payment-method id from Stripe.js, never a card number. Remaining trial is preserved rather than restarted, and an organization that already subscribed once does not get a second free trial.

put.description: Proration is invoiced immediately rather than accruing silently onto the next invoice: an upgrade the customer just chose should not surprise them a month later.

delete.summary: Cancel at the end of the period
delete.description: Access continues until the period ends (the grace period): cancelling must not destroy access already paid for. Use resume to undo.

post.description: Only possible during the grace period; afterwards a new subscription is required.

get.description: Read live from Stripe rather than kept as our own copy of records Stripe owns. Empty for an organization with no Stripe customer.

200.description: Invoices with totals, tax, and download links

get.description: Stream a single invoice PDF.

404.description: Not an invoice of this organization

get.summary: List customers in the active organization
get.description: Paginated list supporting search, filtering, and sorting.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search name, email, company, or phone

403.description: Not a member of the organization, or lacks customers.view

get.summary: One customer, with tags, notes, and attachments

404.description: Not in this organization

put.description: Partial update. Omitting `tags` leaves them unchanged; sending an empty array removes them all.

delete.description: Soft delete: recoverable via the restore endpoint.

404.description: No such deleted customer

get.summary: Export the current selection as CSV
get.description: Streamed and chunked, so a large organization does not exhaust memory. Accepts the same filters as the list endpoint.

post.description: Rows are validated individually: a bad row is reported and skipped rather than aborting the whole file. A `name` column is required.

200.description: Import summary: imported, skipped, and per-row errors

post.summary: Add a note to a customer
post.description: Attach a note to a customer.

get.summary: Aggregate metrics for the active organization
get.description: Customer totals and status breakdown, active-customer lifetime value, a zero-filled six-month growth series, and the five most recent customers. Cached for 5 minutes per organization.

403.description: Not a member, or lacks customers.view

get.summary: Calendar occurrences in a date window
get.description: Returns EXPANDED occurrences, not rows: a recurring series is one row but many occurrences. The window is capped at one year so a malformed rule cannot expand unbounded.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Required. Window end; max one year after from

post.summary: Create an event or recurring series
post.description: Recurrence is stored as a rule and expanded on read. Omit recurrence_frequency for a one-off.

get.summary: One event row with attendees and reminders

404.description: Not in this organization

put.description: Updates the whole series. To change one occurrence only, use the occurrence endpoint.

put.summary: Cancel or move ONE occurrence of a series
put.description: Creates an exception row keyed to the occurrence original start; the expander then skips the generated one. The rest of the series is untouched.

cancel.description: True to remove just this occurrence

get.description: Contents of a folder (root when folder_id is omitted), a breadcrumb built from the materialised path, and a storage summary for the quota bar.

200.description: Folders, files, breadcrumb, and storage usage

post.description: Enforces the plan storage quota (402 when it would be exceeded) and refuses executable or script types regardless of the MIME type claimed. The stored path is generated, never the client filename.

402.description: Would exceed the plan storage quota

422.description: Blocked file type or too large

404.description: Not in this organization

delete.description: Soft delete: the bytes stay until the record is force-deleted, so a restore does not hand back a row pointing at nothing.

delete.summary: Delete a folder and its subtree

post.description: The token is returned only in the URL and stored hashed. The organization slug is part of the link because a public visitor sends no header that could say which tenant database holds the share.

Revokes the current impersonation token immediately — before its own expiry would — and records the end of the session. Returns 409 if the current token is an ordinary login rather than an impersonation session.

200.description: Impersonation ended; the token is now revoked

409.description: The current session is not an impersonation

get.description: Public: the recipient opens the emailed link before signing in, so the SPA must show which organization invited them and to which address. Returns no sensitive fields.

post.description: Outside tenant scope on purpose: the invitee is not a member yet, so the tenant middleware would reject them before they could join. Acceptance is bound to the invited address.

422.description: Invalid token, or issued to a different address

get.description: Each member is shown with the role they hold HERE. The same person may hold a different role in another organization.
Pending invitations for this organization.

post.description: Sends an emailed link with a single-use token valid for 7 days. Re-inviting the same address replaces the previous invitation. Owner can only be granted by an owner.

put.summary: Change a member role in this organization
put.description: An organization must always keep at least one owner; demoting the last one is refused.

404.description: Not a member

422.description: Would leave the organization without an owner

delete.summary: Remove a member from this organization
delete.description: Their role here is revoked; their other organizations are untouched. You cannot remove yourself, nor the last owner.

404.description: Not a member

422.description: Cannot remove yourself or the last owner

get.summary: Your in-app notifications for the active organization
get.description: Notifications live in the tenant database, so the same user has a separate inbox per organization: activity in one org never appears in another.

200.description: Notifications, newest first, plus an unread count

404.description: Not one of your notifications

get.description: Drives the organization switcher. Needs no X-Organization header.

post.description: Provisions a dedicated database, seeds its roles, and makes the caller its owner.
POST rather than PUT because the logo is a multipart upload, which PHP does not parse for PUT. The slug is immutable: it is the tenant identifier clients send in X-Organization.

get.summary: Your role and permissions in the active organization
get.description: The SPA uses the permission list to show or hide controls. The API re-authorizes every request regardless, so this is never the security boundary.
Includes task counts and a progress percentage, loaded with withCount so a list does not fire a query per project.

post.description: Ownership defaults to the creator. Setting status to completed stamps completed_at automatically.

get.summary: One project with members and attachments

404.description: Not in this organization

put.description: status and completed_at are kept in step: marking complete stamps the date, reopening clears it.

delete.description: Tasks are left intact so the project can be restored; cascading would orphan their time entries.

404.description: No such deleted project

get.description: Public. Lets the download page render (and prompt for a password when required) before fetching the bytes. Tenancy is resolved from the organization slug in the path.

200.description: Filename, size, and whether a password is needed

404.description: Unknown organization

422.description: Invalid, expired, or download limit reached

post.description: Public. POST rather than GET so a password can be supplied in the body. Increments the download counter.

password.description: Required when the share is password-protected

422.description: Invalid, expired, limit reached, or wrong password

get.description: The flat, paginated view behind the table and calendar. Use /tasks/board for the Kanban shape. Sorting is restricted to an allowlist of columns rather than passed through to the query.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Allowlisted column; prefix with - to reverse

post.description: A task with a parent_id is a subtask. Creation counts against the plan task quota, which is why the failure here is 402 rather than 403: the caller is permitted, the plan is not.

parent_id.description: Set to make this a subtask

402.description: Would exceed the plan task quota

get.description: Every column is returned even when empty, so the board renders its full shape. One query for the whole board, grouped in PHP.

200.description: Columns in board order: todo, in_progress, review, done

get.summary: One task with subtasks, comments, and time entries

404.description: Not in this organization

put.description: Setting status to done stamps completed_at; moving away from done clears it.

delete.description: Subtasks are soft-deleted with the parent, so they do not resurface at the root of the board.

put.summary: Move a card on the Kanban board
put.description: Positions are floats: a card dropped between two others takes their midpoint, so a drag writes one row rather than renumbering the column. Omit before_id to append to the end.

before_id.description: Task this card is dropped above

post.summary: Start a timer on a task
post.description: A user has at most one running timer; starting a new one stops any other. MySQL has no partial unique index, so this is enforced in the application.

post.summary: Stop your running timer on a task

422.description: No timer running on this task

post.description: Records a completed entry without running a timer. The start is back-dated so the entry occupies real time on a timeline.

get.summary: Your currently running timer, if any
get.description: Drives the persistent timer widget. Returns null when nothing is running.
Entries for a task.

200.description: Entries plus total and billable seconds

delete.description: You may only remove your own time unless you can delete tasks: a timesheet is a billing record, so one person must not quietly rewrite anothers.

403.description: Not your entry and you lack tasks.delete

404.description: Not an entry of this task

get.description: Secrets are never returned here: like an API key, the secret is shown once at creation only.

200.description: Endpoints plus the list of subscribable events

post.description: Returns the signing secret ONCE. Deliveries are signed with HMAC-SHA256 over the exact body in an X-Signature header, so a receiver can prove the payload is ours.

201.description: Created; the response includes the secret

put.description: Re-enabling an endpoint that was auto-paused resets its failure count, so it does not pause again on the next failure regardless of the fix.

get.description: The debugging view: status code, error, and attempt number for the last 50 deliveries.

message.description:
OpenAPI component schema. Never instantiated — a swagger-php annotation holder.
Each reusable schema needs its own class: a class-level #[OA\Schema] means
"this class is the schema", so stacking several on one class registers none.

OrganizationHeader.name: X-Organization
OrganizationHeader.description: Slug or UUID of the organization to act in.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Super Admin platform management across all organizations

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Organizations the caller belongs to, and the active context

tags.name: Team
tags.description: Members, roles, and invitations within the active organization

tags.name: Dashboard
tags.description: Aggregate metrics for the active organization

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: Customer records within the active organization

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Tasks, the Kanban board, and time tracking

tags.name: Files
tags.description: File manager, folders, attachments, and share links
